title: Forms Map
description: Describes the locations of form fields on web pages using CSS selectors. See README.md for full documentation.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version this file conforms to. Must match the version expected by the schema.

hosts.description: Maps hostnames to their form descriptions.

propertyNames.description: Hostname with optional non-default port. Alphanumeric characters, dots, hyphens, underscores, and Unicode characters are allowed. Unicode host keys are normalized to Punycode (ASCII) at build time.

oneOf.description: All pages on this host are irrelevant to the forms concern.

host.description: Form descriptions and pathname-specific overrides for a host.

forms.description: Site-wide fallback form descriptions. Used when no pathname-specific entry matches.

pathnames.description: Maps URL pathnames to page-specific form descriptions. Keys must start with '/'.

forms.description: Form descriptions for this specific pathname.

oneOf.description: This specific page is irrelevant to the forms concern.

propertyNames.description: URL pathname starting with '/'. Must not contain query strings or fragments, and must not have a trailing slash (excepting a sole '/' value itself).

formContent.description: Describes one logical form on a page. 'Form' refers to the user-facing concept and does not require an HTML <form> element.

container.description: Selectors identifying the form's container element on the page. Optional; used to scope the form's fields and actions within the page.

formPurposeCategory.description: Categorical descriptor of the form's purpose.

fieldKey.description: Identifies the user data concept that a form field captures. Keys describe the kind of data (not the input's HTML structure). Each key must be actionable: a consumer should be able to determine what value belongs in the field from the key name and form category alone.

actionKey.description: Identifies actions that belong to the form. These describe structural interactions (not data) that a consumer may need to trigger.

fields.description: Maps field keys to arrays of CSS selectors. Each key identifies the user data concept that a form field captures.

actions.description: Maps action keys to arrays of CSS selectors. Each key identifies a form action or progression element.

selectorArray.description: Alternatives for locating elements of a given concern. The '>>>' combinator indicates boundary crossings (shadow DOM or iframe).

compositeSelectorArray.description: Alternatives for locating elements of a given field concern. Each item is either a single CSS selector (string) or an ordered sequence of selectors (array) representing multiple elements that together compose a single value. The '>>>' combinator indicates boundary crossings (shadow DOM or iframe).

deepSelectorSequence.description: An ordered sequence of CSS selectors representing multiple elements that together compose a single value for the field. Order is significant; the map does not specify how the value is split across the elements.

deepSelector.description: A CSS selector, optionally using '>>>' to pierce shadow DOM or iframe boundaries.
